capstone-tests-and-polish.summary: Finish with tests, clean output, and documentation for a runnable OOP application.

try_capstone_tests_and_polish_sketch1.title: Try it yourself
try_capstone_tests_and_polish_sketch1.prompt: Write an integration test for loading rows and rendering a summary report.

ci-capstone-polish-practice.title: Polish capstone output and README
ci-capstone-polish-practice.prompt: Complete final polish by writing a summary file and keeping README in the project.
ci-capstone-polish-practice.hint: Read the imports first, then put each responsibility in the file named by that import.

help.concept: In a multi-file project, each file should own one clear responsibility and imports should connect those responsibilities.
help.hint_1: Run from the entry file and follow the first failing import or assertion.
help.hint_2: Keep the full project state working, not just the one line you changed.

ci-capstone-polish-practice.starterCode:
from services.reports import save_summary

save_summary("reports/summary.txt", "Ready")
print(open("reports/summary.txt").read())

sc-capstone-tests-and-polish-design.title: Check the design choice
sc-capstone-tests-and-polish-design.prompt: Which choice best matches the lesson goal for capstone tests and polish?
sc-capstone-tests-and-polish-design.hint: Choose the option that keeps object responsibilities clear.

help.concept: Finish with tests, clean output, and documentation for a runnable OOP application.
help.hint_1: Look for the answer that reduces duplication without hiding behavior.
help.hint_2: A good OOP answer usually keeps behavior near the object that owns the data.

options.a: Put every class in main.py so imports are never needed.
options.b: Give each class or service a focused responsibility and import it where needed.
options.c: Avoid methods so every object exposes all of its data directly.
options.d: Copy the same logic into each subclass to avoid shared code.

try-capstone-tests-and-polish-sketch0.title: Test the capstone models
try-capstone-tests-and-polish-sketch0.prompt: Write assertions for bug and feature labels.
try-capstone-tests-and-polish-sketch0.hint: Read the imports first, then put each responsibility in the file named by that import.

help.concept: In a multi-file project, each file should own one clear responsibility and imports should connect those responsibilities.
help.hint_1: Run from the entry file and follow the first failing import or assertion.
help.hint_2: Keep the full project state working, not just the one line you changed.

try-capstone-tests-and-polish-sketch0.starterCode:
import sys
sys.path.insert(0, ".")

from models.bug_task import BugTask
from models.feature_task import FeatureTask

# add assertions
print("model tests passed")

try-capstone-tests-and-polish-sketch1.title: Test storage and reporting together
try-capstone-tests-and-polish-sketch1.prompt: Write an integration test for loading rows and rendering a summary report.
try-capstone-tests-and-polish-sketch1.hint: Read the imports first, then put each responsibility in the file named by that import.

help.concept: In a multi-file project, each file should own one clear responsibility and imports should connect those responsibilities.
help.hint_1: Run from the entry file and follow the first failing import or assertion.
help.hint_2: Keep the full project state working, not just the one line you changed.

try-capstone-tests-and-polish-sketch1.starterCode:
import sys
sys.path.insert(0, ".")

from services.storage import load_tasks
from services.reports import summary_report

# add integration assertions
print("app tests passed")

concept.title: Capstone Tests and Polish: the idea
concept.bodyMarkdown: **Capstone Tests and Polish** is not just syntax. In this course it is practiced inside small projects where one file owns one clear part of the program. Finish with tests, clean output, and documentation for a runnable OOP application.

multifile-shape.title: Capstone Tests and Polish: the file shape
multifile-shape.bodyMarkdown: The file layout matters. Models hold object state and behavior, services coordinate groups of objects, tests verify behavior, and `main.py` stays thin enough to read.

graded-habit.title: Capstone Tests and Polish: what the grader checks
graded-habit.bodyMarkdown: The grader checks behavior from the running project. A strong answer keeps imports working, keeps all files present, and proves the object behavior through output or assertions.
